Plan name: United Nations Sustainable Development Cooperation Framework
Strategic priority: Transformação Económica Inclusiva, Prosperidade e Transição Justa para um Ambiente Saudável / Inclusive Economic Transformation, Prosperity, and Just Transition to a Healthy Environment
Outcome: Efeito 2.1: Até 2027, uma economia mais sustentável, inclusiva, diversificada e integrada gerará empregos decentes, segurança alimentar e nutrição para os mais vulneráveis, especialmente as mulheres, os jovens e os mais pobres. / Outcome 2.1: By 2027, a more sustainable, inclusive, diversified and integrated economy generates decent jobs, food security and nutrition for the most vulnerable, especially women, youth and the extreme poor.
Output: Produto 2.1.1: As estruturas institucionais são reforçadas e mais inclusivas, e os responsáveis ​​têm capacidades fortalecidas para um melhor ambiente de negócios, sistemas nacionais de qualidade, integração regional, uma maior contribuição da diáspora para a economia e a proteção dos direitos dos trabalhadores.
Sub-Output: Fontes de receita municipal e introdução  de taxas de serviços relacionados com resíduos e mecanismos de fiscalização avaliados
Description: Será realizada uma avaliação detalhada das receitas locais com incidência nos quadros jurídicos, práticas institucionais e sistemas de dados, visando identificar o potencial inexplorado e lacunas no registo, avaliação, conformidade e fiscalização no município da Boa Vista. A avaliação culminará em recomendações para informar estratégias de aumento de receitas, incluindo potenciais taxas relacionadas com os resíduos. O objetivo geral é garantir que a Câmara Municipal da Boa Vista terá capacidade financeira para participar de forma sustentável na Parceria Público Privada.
Joint Programmes / Initiatives names: Building public-private partnerships to boost circular economy on the tourist island of Boa Vista - Cabo Verde

Gender marker: 1 - Contributes to gender equality/women's empowerment in a limited way

A melhoria dos serviços de resíduos reduz cargas ambientais e sanitárias que afetam de forma desproporcional as mulheres e raparigas nos domicílios.

Plan name: United Nations Sustainable Development Cooperation Framework

Outcome: Efeito 2.2: Até 2027, os principais ecossistemas marinhos e terrestres e a biodiversidade estarão melhor protegidos, restaurados e geridos de forma mais sustentável, e a resiliência aos choques e impactos das alterações climáticas será melhorada e mais sensíveis às questões do género. / Outcome 2.2: By 2027, key marine and land ecosystems and biodiversity are better protected, restored, and more sustainably managed and the resilience to shocks and climate change are improved and gender responsive.
Output: Produto 2.2.3: Os quadros jurídicos e de planeamento são melhorados e melhor implementados com abordagens participativas na gestão do ambiente e dos recursos naturais, incluindo energia limpa acessível e preparação e resposta a choques.
Suboutput also contributes to the follow: 2.1.1 Produto 2.1.1: As estruturas institucionais são reforçadas e mais inclusivas, e os responsáveis ​​têm capacidades fortalecidas para um melhor ambiente de negócios, sistemas nacionais de qualidade, integração regional, uma maior contribuição da diáspora para a economia e a proteção dos direitos dos trabalhadores.; 1.1.1 Produto 1.1.1: As instituições nacionais, os parceiros sociais e as organizações da sociedade civil, têm maiores e melhores capacidades para fornecer, inovar e fazer parcerias para a protecção social e a proteção das crianças e adolescentes, os serviços essenciais, e sistemas de dados, monitorização e informação.; 2.1.2 Produto 2.1.2: As pequenas e médias empresas são integradas nas cadeias verde, azul e de valor, suas capacidades produtivas e acesso a serviços financeiros e não financeiros são fortalecidos e soluções de mercado, baseadas na natureza e com eficiência energética (no setor privado) são promovidos.
Sub-Output: Plano de melhoria da gestão de resíduos elaborado e adotado com base nas conclusões da ferramenta Waste Wise Cities.
Description: Com base na ferramenta Waste Wise Cities, será realizada uma avaliação do sistema de resíduos sólidos da ilha da Boa Vista, abrangendo a geração de resíduos, a recolha, as práticas de eliminação e a fuga para o ambiente. A avaliação produzirá dados que servirão de base para (i) o estudo de viabilidade para a criação de uma PPP e promover a economia circular na ilha; (ii) a adoção de medidas destinadas a melhorar a prestação de serviços, a recuperação de recursos e minimizar impactos no ambiente.

Human rights marker narrative: Na perspectiva que esta atividade impede a violacao dos DH atraves do reforco da integriade e combate a corrupcao.
2023 Narrative: Com foco na prevenção e combate à corrupção, o ONUDC em Cabo Verde apoiou as seguintes iniciativas:Formação Avançada online sobre Prevenção e Combate à Corrupção da Administração Pública para os membros do Conselho da Prevenção da Corrupção (10) junto do Tribunal de Contas, de 19 de abril e 18 de maio, para reforcar o conhecimento de temas essenciais de corrupção e integridade, tais como Convenção das Nações Unidas contra a Corrupção, o regime geral de prevenção da corrupção e a gestão do risco de lavagem de capitais, financiamento do terrorismo, corrupção e fraude económico-financeira. Segundo os participantes, a formação revelou-se ser de grande utilidade, uma vez que os formandos adquiriram conhecimentos que contemplam não somente as especificidades que integram os procedimentos a serem adotados, segundo as melhores práticas, na prevenção e combate à corrupção e crimes conexos, como também proporcionou absorção de conceitos primários sobre a elaboração de planos de prevenção e combate à corrupção, bem como códigos de boas práticas e de conduta, tudo numa perspetiva de diminuir ao máximo os riscos de ocorrência do fenómeno da corrupção em Cabo Verde, tanto no setor público como nas entidades privadas.Conferência Internacional Sobre a Ética e Integridade dos Magistrados do Ministério Público e Aprovação da Proposta de Código de Ética dos Magistrados do Ministério Público de Cabo Verde, visando a promoção de debates sobre o tema e a adoção pelo coletivo de procuradores caboverdianos de um Código de Ética. II Encontro Anual de juízes caboverdianos, realizada na cidade da Praia nos dias 20 e 21 de outubro, sob o tema “Tribunais e Comunicação social no combate à Desinformação e no reforço à Integridade”, em que cerca de 85 membros da comunidade jurídica e da CPLP tiveram a oportunidade de reforçar o intercâmbio de conhecimento e partilhas de experiências sobre os desafios inerentes à relação entre a comunicação social e os tribunais; Reprodução dos códigos de ética do Ministério Público e da Magistratura Judicial;Formação de formadores sobre ética e integridade dirigida a 9 Investigadores da Polícia Judiciária e 10 da Polícia Nacional que, por sua vez, participaram como formadores de cerca de um total de 35 policiais na mesma área temática.

2024 Narrative: The first international underwater archaeology field school was a major activity in Cabo Verde, raising awareness of the need to protect underwater cultural assets, collecting valuable data for understanding the history of Cabo Verde, and training 12 young people from 8 African countries in underwater archaeology. 2023 Narrative: To support national efforts for the promotion and application of the ILO MNE Declaration in Cabo Verde and to stimulate a dialogue on the role of multinational enterprises in achieving decent work in the tourism sector, an Atelier "The Tripartite Declaration of Principles on Multinational Enterprises and Tourism's contribution to achieving the Sustainable Development Goals" was organized on October 26 and 27, 2022. This Forum resulted in an indicative road map to be undertaken for a better contribution of companies operating in the tourism sector to implement the decent work agenda. One of the central recommendations is creating a tripartite working group within the National Tourism Council, an advisory body on tourism sector policy, composed of representatives of the different subsectors of the respective economic activity, whose function is to advise the member of the Government responsible for the sector.To leverage the results of the workshop "The Tripartite Declaration of Principles on Multinational Enterprises and Tourism's Contribution to Achieving the Sustainable Development Goals", a consultancy, based on dialogue with ILO tripartite constituents and sector stakeholders, was launched to refine and finalize the roadmap resulting from the workshop, as well as to define the next steps and draft the terms of reference for the establishment of a working group for the promotion of decent work in the tourism sector.A session on Business HelpDesk, a one-stop shop for any questions related to responsible business conduct and the application of International Labor Standards took place on the 20th of June, was led by the MULTI department, and mobilized various stakeholders. Following the session, employers promoted the ILO Business Helpdesk on their websites and other media/social network:https://www.facebook.com/photo/?fbid=986971175975239set=a.745851126753913A follow-up session to the workshop "Achieving decent work for all - Guidelines of the MNE Declaration" was organized in August. During this last session, the recommendations made in the various forums were discussed in depth, namely the appointment of tripartite national focal points, as an operational instrument of the MNE Declaration.The following representation proposal was considered, for a 4-year term.- A focal point for the Ministry of Labor- One focal point representing employers- One focal point representing the CCSL- One focal point representing the UNTC-CS- One focal point representing Cabo Verde TradeInvest.The efforts of the national focal points to actively promote the principles of the MNE Declaration at the country level may include: raising awareness among government ministries and agencies, multinational enterprises, and employers' and workers' organizations of the Declaration's principles on multinational enterprises; organizing capacity-building events; and developing online information and dialogue platforms in local languages, where possible.National focal points may also wish to organize extended tripartite dialogue platforms for tripartite constituents and MNEs to discuss opportunities and identify challenges presented by MNE operations in the national context. Such dialogues could draw on past experiences, lessons learned, and best practices; they could also encompass dialogues between home and host countries, as described in paragraph 12 of the Declaration. It was agreed that the appointment should be made official through a ministerial order, based on the nominations of the entities that will form part of the focal points group. Bearing in mind that the Addis Ababa Action Agenda (2015) on financing for development underlines the importance of international trade and investment as a means of promoting productive employment, and decent work and contributing to the achievement of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), the project facilitated the participation of the Foreign Investment Agency in the course “Multinational enterprises, development and decent work: The approach of the MNE Declaration” (ITC-ILO)In December 2023, a hybrid session was organized with the social partners to promote the MNE Declaration. On the occasion of the awareness-raising workshop organized in 2022, the national tripartite constituents developed a roadmap for the promotion and application of the Declaration and expressed interest in appointing national focal points. The session aimed to follow up on the workshop and build a common understanding of the priorities and next steps for promoting the MNE Declaration in the country, using a tripartite approach.
2024 Narrative: The project has relied on communication materials to make the principles set out in the ILO Tripartite Declaration of Principles concerning Multinational Enterprises and Social Policy (MNE Declaration), more accessible and easier to consult for all those interested.  The project facilitated the participation of the Cabo Verde Trade Invest focal point for the MNE Declaration in the EFFECTIVE INVESTMENT FACILITATION AND SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENT course, organized by the Turin International Foundation Centre - https://www.itcilo.org/courses/effective-investment-facilitation-and-sustainable-development-2023 - and at the Tripartite Workshop “Multinational Enterprises, Development, and Decent Work: The Application of the ILO Tripartite Declaration on Multinational Enterprises and Social Policy” (Lisbon, October 2024).To support national efforts for the promotion and application of the ILO MNE Declaration in Cabo Verde and to stimulate a dialogue on the role of multinational enterprises in achieving decent work, a sensitization session for the fish-processing industry on the ILO MNE Declaration was organized on 5 September 2022, in São Vicente, to promote the principles of the MNE declaration and also to increase social and economic benefits of the sustainable use of resources marine (SDG 14.7). This workshop resulted in an indicative road map to be undertaken for a better contribution of companies operating in the fish-processing industry to implement a decent work agenda.To leverage these results, a consultancy is in progress based on dialogue to consult with the ILO tripartite constituents and the sectoral stakeholders to refine and finalize the road map resulting from the workshop and to lay down the next steps and draft terms of reference for the creation of a Working group for the promotion of decent work in the fish-processing industry. The Forum "The Tripartite Declaration of Principles on Multinational Enterprises and Tourism's Contribution to Achieving the Sustainable Development Goals" (Sal, October 2022) resulted in an indicative road map to be undertaken for a better contribution of companies operating in the tourism sector to the implementation of the decent work agenda. The consolidated version of the Roadmap for the Promotion of the ILO Tripartite Declaration of Principles concerning Enterprises and Social Policy in the Tourism Sector in Cape Verde was adopted on December 28, 2023.In May and June 2024, consultation sessions were held with the members of the Tourism Sector Platform, to consolidate the governance mechanism. During these working sessions, it was decided that the leadership of the platform would be the responsibility of the Cape Verde Chamber of Tourism.Among the actions foreseen in the national roadmap to promote the MNE Declaration as direct guidance to enterprises or sectors, are included its wide dissemination in various forums, in particular the Cabo Verde Trade Fair (FIC) - https://fic.cv/The FIC holds significant relevance for various stakeholders, enhancing business visibility. The fair serves as a crucial platform to showcase governmental support for trade, investment, and business development initiatives, signaling a commitment to fostering a conducive environment for economic activities. It provides opportunities for tourism companies, travel agencies, and hospitality businesses to connect, facilitating partnerships and collaborations that can enhance tourism offerings.Thus, under the leadership of the Chamber of Tourism, the project took part in the FIC 2024 intending to promote the MNE Declaration, through seminars, and the dissemination of brochures such as the ILO Helpdesk for Business on International Labour Standards, and the text of the Declaration itself (publications available in Portuguese, printed).Considering that the MNE Declaration emphasizes the central role of freedom of association and collective bargaining of freedom of association and collective bargaining, labor relations, and social dialogue, a session on the MNE Declaration was included in the agenda for the training of trainers on the transition from the informal to the formal economy (Project Form@Empresa, Praia, November 2024)Based on the ACTRAV´s guidance document “The ILO MNE Declaration: What’s in it for Workers?”, an online training course for workers' representatives is in progress to provide a forward-looking view on approaches and pledges how workers and trade unions can successfully use the MNE Declaration in practice. The Training course for workers' representatives is designed to encourage dissemination of the MNE Declaration, its approach, principles, and operational tools through trade union structures, and voluntary negotiations between employer organizations and employee organizations.

2023 Narrative: A Estratégia Nacional de Cabo Verde de Prevenção e Combate à Lavagem de Capitais, ao Financiamento do Terrorismo e ao Financiamento da Proliferação de Armas de Destruição Maciça (ENCAVE) foi aprovada juntamente com o Plano Operacional 2023, a 6 de fevereiro de 2023, e apresentada ao Conselho de Ministros a 28 de março de 2023. A ENCAVE foi aprovada pelo Governo e publicada no Boletim Oficial a 3 de abril de 2023. A ENCAVE é um documento de referência para a prevenção e o combate ao branqueamento de capitais, ao financiamento do terrorismo e ao financiamento da proliferação de armas de destruição maciça em Cabo Verde.A ENCAVE constitui uma base de referência para os diversos actores no domínio da prevenção e combate ao branqueamento de capitais, ao financiamento do terrorismo e à proliferação de armas, e inclui acções a desenvolver pelos organismos e instituições que integram o sector público, mas também o sector privado, as ONG e a sociedade civil. Realização de formação especializada em recolha e partilha de informações financeiras, com ferramentas da INTERPOL, ministrada por especialistas da INTERPOL, de 23 a 26 de outubro, na cidade da Praia, abrangeu 41 atores, dos quais 17 mulheres, com responsabilidade ao nível da recolha, do tratamento e da investigação de informações financeiras ( 2 representantes da Unidade de Informação Financeira, 5 supervisores do Banco de Cabo Verde, 15 investigadores e analistas da Polícia Judiciária, 15 Procuradores e um juiz), com os objetivos principais de permitir aos participantes o intercâmbio das melhores práticas em matéria de recolha e partilha de informações financeiras e deforçar a capacidade nacional de prevenir o abuso do sistema financeiro, no ambito do do projeto CRIMJUST (Segmento Cabo Verde).

Sub-Output: Accelerating the eradication of extreme poverty in Cabo Verde through economic inclusion
Description:
PDO: To accelerate the eradication of extreme poverty in Cabo Verde, by reinforcing the economic inclusion and 
income stability of the extreme poor, through access labour markets and self-employment opportunities. The 
economic inclusion of households living in rural areas will be part of the objective, as well as businesses operating in 
the informal sector.
Key results: Individuals from households living in extreme poverty, will:
(1) enhance their sills to access decent work opportunities, especially women and youth NEET, 
(2) those living in rural areas will benefit from support to enhance the productivity of their micro-business and selfemployment activities;
(3) micro-businesses operating in the informal sector will benefit from support to transition to the formal sector.
Joint Programmes / Initiatives names: Accelerating the eradication of extreme poverty in Cabo Verde through economic inclusion
Implementation partners: MFIDS - Ministério da Família, Inclusão e Desenvolvimento Social
SDG Targets: 1.1 By 2030, eradicate extreme poverty for all people everywhere, currently measured as people living on less than $1.25 a day.,1.2 By 2030, reduce at least by half the proportion of men, women and children of all ages living in poverty in all its dimensions according to national definitions.,1.3 Implement nationally appropriate social protection systems and measures for all, including floors, and by 2030 achieve substantial coverage of the poor and the vulnerable.,2.1 By 2030, end hunger and ensure access by all people, in particular the poor and people in vulnerable situations, including infants, to safe, nutritious and sufficient food all year round.,5.1 End all forms of discrimination against all women and girls everywhere.,8.3 Promote development-oriented policies that support productive activities, decent job creation, entrepreneurship, creativity and innovation, and encourage the formalization and growth of micro-, small- and medium-sized enterprises, including through access to financial services.,8.6 By 2020, substantially reduce the proportion of youth not in employment, education or training.
SDG Goals: 1 No Poverty; 2 Zero Hunger; 5 Gender Equality; 8 Decent Jobs and Economic Growth
QCPR function: Data Collection and Analysis; Capacity Development/Technical Assistance; Direct Support/ Service Delivery
Gender marker: 2 - Gender equality/women's empowerment is a significant objective
Human rights marker: 2 - Significant contribution to realization of human rights
2025 Narrative: UNDP within the JP supported:Output 2: Strengthening skills (for youth and women) and institutional capacity to facilitate access to decent employment The JP contributed to UNCF Outcome 2. 1 by placing strong emphasis on youth empowerment as a driver of sustainable development and significantly advanced youth engagement and economic empowerment initiatives in Cabo Verde, leveraging innovative programs and mechanisms to expand opportunities for young people as well as allowing strengthening the role of young people as actors and protagonists in the country's developmentIn this regard, a training package for youth NEETS , especially those in extreme poverty, was organized on Financial Education and Project Management. This initiative allowed young people to enhance competencies by providing practical tools and techniques for the development of sustainable and high-impact projects, as well as for improved financial management and project sustainability. Capacity building for 101 young people — 45 men and 56 women, representing youth associations were reinforced resulting in 30 community inclusion projects developed and implemented, which created around 45 direct youth jobs. This capacity-building initiative has contributed to enhancing youth employability and entrepreneurship, promoting economic autonomy, community leadership, and sustainable social transformation, reaching over 300 indirect beneficiariesAlso, in partnership with BIC - Business Incubator Center, the 'Impactar' programme was launched Through an interactive Bootcamp approach. IMPACTAR equipped youth—particularly vulnerable groups such as women and persons with disabilities—with entrepreneurial skills to foster innovation and self-employment. The results were tangible: 100 youth benefited – (55 women, 45 men), 30 youth -led projects were implemented, contributing to reducing youth unemployment and alleviating extreme poverty;The JP also supported the Prevention and reduction of urban violence and juvenile delinquency by enhancing employability and socio-professional integration among young people in situations of social and/or judicial vulnerability. 40 young people successfully completed technical training programs in Drywall Installation (Pladur) and Plumbing and Building Systems. These interventions have strengthened personal, social, and professional competencies, generating tangible pathways for socio-economic inclusion.The JP provides 30 prizes for the best youth business plans to facilitate the creation of business units and 30 youth -led projects were implemented, and these generated over 75 jobs, directly contributing to reducing youth unemployment and alleviating extreme poverty. This initiative has contributed to enhancing youth employability and entrepreneurship, promoting economic autonomy, community leadership, and sustainable social transformation, reaching over 300 indirect beneficiaries.Output 4: Strengthening of institutional capacity of MFIDS to monitor and assess social programmes The JP provided Capacity building to MFIDS (Ministry for family, inclusion and social development) and the 22 Municipalities team that allowed to improve the implementation of social programs, and the management of the CSU- single social registry, contributing to the UNCF Outcome 1, namely:He JP supported the expansion of the government social protection program, which allowed the country to establish a single social registry – CSU updated and contributing to the implementation and allocation of social programs to the poor and most vulnerable population, promoting their access to an integrated social protection. Specifically: 2,779 new households were registered, corresponding to 7,955 individuals. Additionally, 31,475 record updates were carried out. Plan name: United Nations Sustainable Development Cooperation Framework
Strategic priority: Reforço do Talento Humano e do Capital Social / Strengthened Human Talent and Social Capital
Outcome: Efeito 1: Até 2027, mais pessoas, especialmente as que vivem em situações de mais vulnerabilidade ou marginalização e as que vivem com deficiências, terão acesso equitativo e utilização de serviços sociais essenciais e de proteção de alta qualidade, resilientes, sensíveis ao género e modernos. / Outcome 1: By 2027, more people, especially those living in the most vulnerable and marginalized situations and those living with disabilities, have equitable access to and use of high-quality, resilient, gender-responsive, and modern essential social services and protection.
Output: Produto 1.1.1: As instituições nacionais, os parceiros sociais e as organizações da sociedade civil, têm maiores e melhores capacidades para fornecer, inovar e fazer parcerias para a protecção social e a proteção das crianças e adolescentes, os serviços essenciais, e sistemas de dados, monitorização e informação.
Suboutput also contributes to the follow: 1.1.1 Produto 1.1.1: As instituições nacionais, os parceiros sociais e as organizações da sociedade civil, têm maiores e melhores capacidades para fornecer, inovar e fazer parcerias para a protecção social e a proteção das crianças e adolescentes, os serviços essenciais, e sistemas de dados, monitorização e informação.
Sub-Output: Supporting the preparation of the  roadmap for the GA in Cabo Verde and  feasibility studies to accelerate the  eradication of extreme poverty at  municipal level through decent jobs and  social protection
Description:
The project aims to:
(1) support the Government of Cabo Verde in the preparation of its roadmap for 
the GA. The country joined the GA as a pathfinder country on January 24th
, 2024,
and is currently in the process of preparing the national roadmap. With the 
support of UNCT, the Government has initiated the mapping of social protection 
and employment national plans and the identification of constraints that could 
benefit from external support. This project would support further analysis of the 
opportunities that the GA can offer and the challenges it is expected to tackle, as 
well as the preparation of a participatory diagnostic and the final drafting of the 
roadmap.
(2) support the Government of Cabo Verde, through the preparation of feasibility 
studies, to design a strategy to articulate and better integrate the different 
national social protection and employment plans at the municipal level. Cabo 
Verde’s central priority and commitment to eradicating extreme poverty by 2026
will require further integration of these sectoral plans and policies, taking into 
consideration concrete gaps in different municipalities. The ultimate goal is to 
ensure the sustainability of the current national strategy to eradicate extreme 
poverty, including by building institutional capacity for its implementation
Joint Programmes / Initiatives names: Supporting the preparation of the roadmap for the GA in Cabo Verde and feasibility studies to accelerate the eradication of extreme poverty at municipal level through decent jobs and social protection
Implementation partners: MFIDS - Ministério da Família, Inclusão e Desenvolvimento Social
SDG Targets: 1.1 By 2030, eradicate extreme poverty for all people everywhere, currently measured as people living on less than $1.25 a day.,1.2 By 2030, reduce at least by half the proportion of men, women and children of all ages living in poverty in all its dimensions according to national definitions.,1.3 Implement nationally appropriate social protection systems and measures for all, including floors, and by 2030 achieve substantial coverage of the poor and the vulnerable.,2.1 By 2030, end hunger and ensure access by all people, in particular the poor and people in vulnerable situations, including infants, to safe, nutritious and sufficient food all year round.,5.1 End all forms of discrimination against all women and girls everywhere.,8.3 Promote development-oriented policies that support productive activities, decent job creation, entrepreneurship, creativity and innovation, and encourage the formalization and growth of micro-, small- and medium-sized enterprises, including through access to financial services.,8.6 By 2020, substantially reduce the proportion of youth not in employment, education or training.
SDG Goals: 1 No Poverty; 2 Zero Hunger; 5 Gender Equality; 8 Decent Jobs and Economic Growth
QCPR function: Convening/Partnerships/Knowledge Sharing; Capacity Development/Technical Assistance; Data Collection and Analysis
Gender marker: 2 - Gender equality/women's empowerment is a significant objective
Human rights marker: 2 - Significant contribution to realization of human rights
2025 Narrative: In complementary with UNDP and UNICEF activities, the JP supported the implementation of the government social protection program, which allowed the country to establish a single social registry – CSU updated and contributing to the implementation and allocation of social programs to the poor and most vulnerable population, promoting their access to an integrated social protection. Specifically, around 2,779 new households were registered, corresponding to 7,955 individuals. Additionally,31,475 record updates were carried out. These efforts resulted in the qualification of 2,135 new beneficiaries for the Social Inclusion Income Program (RSI) and 1,474 beneficiaries for the Productive Inclusion Program (IP), contributing to the reduction of extreme poverty, which stands at a rate of 2.28%. On the other hand, the communication campaign for the registration and update process of the CSU, with the production and dissemination of communication materials, contributed to strengthening the institutional communication of the SingleSocial Registry (CSU), increasing public recognition and trust in its mission to support the management of social policies in an integrated and transparent way. The technical and financial support from UNDP through this JP also allowed for improvements and adjustments in the management tools of the CSU System, promoting the modernization and continuous enhancement of its management tools, as well as ensuring greater efficiency, integrity, and security in the processes of registration, control, and monitoring of beneficiaries of social programs. Technical capacities at central and local level were strengthened in the country’s 22 municipalities, which allowed the successful updating of the CSU.
